name: Hampshire College Summer Studies in Mathematics

cost.notes: Need-based financial aid available; full scholarships for qualifying students

essays.prompt: Describe a mathematical concept or problem that you find beautiful and explain why.
tips: HCSSIM values authentic reflection over impressive-sounding answers
Be specific about what you actually find beautiful in math

outcomes.college_impact: Positive signal at selective colleges; alumni are well-prepared for college math.
outcomes.typical_results: Most alumni pursue STEM fields; strong placement at liberal arts colleges and research universities.

what_they_look_for: Willingness to struggle with hard problems

blurbs.short: A 6-week residential math program at Hampshire College where students tackle challenging problem sets and explore advanced topics.
blurbs.long: HCSSIM (Hampshire College Summer Studies in Mathematics) is one of the original elite math summer programs, following the discovery-learning philosophy pioneered by Ross and PROMYS. Students spend six weeks working through a sequence of challenging problems, attending lectures, and developing as mathematical thinkers. What sets HCSSIM apart is its integration with Hampshire College's non-traditional, interdisciplinary approach to education — students are encouraged to think broadly and make connections across fields. The community is tight-knit (only 40 students per year) and many alumni stay connected for years after the program.

curriculum: Problem sets in advanced topics, lectures, problem-solving workshops, student presentations.
